display.purpose: Hero and page headlines only; tight tracking, never above ~3.75rem.

label.purpose: JetBrains Mono uppercase: eyebrows, nav, chips, versions, code, stats.

motion.name: hover-lift
motion.purpose: Subtle hover feedback on interactive cards and buttons; collapses to none under prefers-reduced-motion.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one inverted-fill CTA per view.

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary action: transparent with a line border that lifts to paper/40 on hover.

components.name: Catalog Card
components.description: Graphite surface, 1px line border, flat; border lifts to paper/40 on hover.
components.html:
Redis

In-memory data store, hardened and built from source to zero fixable CVEs.

components.name: Metadata Chip
components.description: Mono uppercase tag for category / tier / license.

components.name: CVE Status Chip
components.description: Signature component: leads with the Trivy letter grade, count alongside; functional color (amber shown) always backed by the grade text and an aria-label.
components.html: C 13

components.name: Nav Link
components.description: Mono uppercase link; ash at rest, paper on hover/active.

narrative.overview: QuenchWorks is a verifiable catalog, not a sales page, and the system reads like an audit trail: every claim sits next to the artifact that proves it — a sha256 digest, a Trivy grade, a cosign signature, a live CVE count. The surface is a dark, matte, terminal-native monochrome; energy comes from high contrast, decisive typography, and tabular precision, never from hue. It is built for a skeptical platform engineer with a terminal open in the next tab, so it commits to restraint as a credibility signal: the absence of hype IS the positioning.
keyCharacteristics: Monochrome ink + paper + neutral ramp; color appears only to encode security severity.
Inter for structure, JetBrains Mono for every piece of metadata, code, and label.
Flat by default — depth comes from tonal surfaces and 1px borders, never shadow.
Tabular figures everywhere numbers appear (versions, CVE counts, sizes).
WCAG-AA verified; keyboard, reduced-motion, increased-contrast, and RTL all first-class.

rules.name: The Monochrome Rule
rules.body: The brand is ink + paper + the neutral ramp. No decorative hue, no brand accent color. Energy is contrast and type, never color.

rules.name: The Functional-Color-Only Rule
rules.body: The emerald/amber/red trio appears ONLY to encode CVE severity, and never as the sole signal — the letter grade and an aria-label always carry the same meaning as text.

rules.name: The Mono-for-Metadata Rule
rules.body: JetBrains Mono is reserved for machine-adjacent text: versions, digests, CVE counts, labels, nav, code. Prose is always Inter.

rules.name: The Tabular Rule
rules.body: Any digit that could shift between renders uses tabular figures so columns never jitter.

rules.name: The Border-Not-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Separation is a 1px line border and a tonal step (ink to graphite), never a box-shadow.

rules.name: The State-Reveals-Depth Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest; the only lift is a state change (hover shifts a card border from line to paper/40).

dos: Do keep the surface monochrome (ink/graphite/line/ash/paper); carry energy with contrast and type.
Do make the CTA an inverted paper-on-ink fill, used once per view; everything else is ghost or text.
Do draw separation with full 1px line borders and tonal steps; keep surfaces flat.
Do set all metadata (versions, digests, CVE counts, labels) in JetBrains Mono with tabular figures.
Do back every status color with a text grade + aria-label; honor prefers-reduced-motion and prefers-contrast.
donts: Don't introduce a decorative brand accent or any hue that isn't encoding security severity.
Don't use gradient text, decorative glassmorphism, or neon-on-black (the crypto/AI-hype-dark cliché).
Don't ship a generic-SaaS hero: no gradient hero, no cream/sand background, no hero-metric template, no identical icon-card grids, no uppercase eyebrow above every section.
Don't adopt the corporate/enterprise-vendor look QuenchWorks replaces — no heavy chrome, stock imagery, or 'contact sales'.
Don't go playful/consumer — no mascots, bright multi-color, or illustration-heavy warmth.
Don't use a colored border-left/right side-stripe, add drop shadows for depth, or nest cards inside cards.
